setting.note: Pip's road runs from the Kent marshes to London and back. Dickens lightly fictionalises the marsh village and "the town", but draws them from real ground: the churchyard at Cooling on the Hoo peninsula, and Rochester, whose Restoration House became Satis House. In London the places are named outright, Little Britain and Smithfield, Newgate, Barnard's Inn. The bbox spans the whole run, from the estuary marshes east of Rochester to Holborn.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Charles Dickens, first published 1861; the author died in 1870.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1870).
rights.attribution: Charles Dickens, Great Expectations (1861). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The red of Joe's forge fire, the warm hearth Pip leaves behind for London, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

pip.name: Pip
pip.colorSourceQuote: the low leaden line beyond was the river
